text: The Gradient Descent parameter update formula is {{c1::\(\theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta \nabla J(\theta_t)\)}}
explanation: This formula iteratively adjusts model parameters (weights) \(\theta\) to minimize the loss function \(J(\theta)\). The gradient \(\nabla J(\theta_t)\) points in the direction of steepest increase, so subtracting it steps down toward the minimum, scaled by learning rate \(\eta\).
usage:
Core formula breakdown:
  • \(\theta\): Parameter weight vector
  • \(\eta\): Learning rate (determines step size)
  • \(J(\theta)\): Loss/objective function
  • \(\nabla J(\theta_t)\): Gradient vector at time step t
spanish: La fórmula de actualización de parámetros en el Descenso de Gradiente es \(\theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta \nabla J(\theta_t)\).

text: The Adam optimizer calculates adaptive learning rates by combining {{c1::Momentum}} (moving average of past gradients) and {{c1::RMSProp}} (moving average of past squared gradients).
explanation: Adam (Adaptive Moment Estimation) tracks both the first moment (mean of gradients, similar to Momentum) and the second moment (uncentered variance of gradients, similar to RMSProp) to scale steps independently for each weight.
usage:
Mathematical concept:
  • Momentum term helps speed up optimization in direction of consistent gradients.
  • RMSProp term dampens updates in highly volatile directions, preventing exploding parameters.
spanish: El optimizador Adam calcula tasas de aprendizaje adaptativas combinando Momentum (promedio móvil de gradientes pasados) y RMSProp (promedio móvil de gradientes pasados al cuadrado).

text: To predict continuous outputs (regression) or discrete categories (classification), we train models using {{c1::supervised learning}} on labeled data.
explanation: Supervised learning algorithms learn a mapping function \(f(x) = y\) from training pairs \((x, y)\) containing input features and corresponding ground-truth targets.
usage: Examples of supervised learning: Linear Regression, Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Support Vector Machines.
spanish: Para predecir salidas continuas (regresión) o categorías discretas (clasificación), entrenamos modelos utilizando aprendizaje supervisado (supervised learning) en datos etiquetados.

text: To discover hidden structures, groupings, or reduce dimensions without utilizing ground-truth labels, you apply {{c1::unsupervised learning}}.
explanation: Unsupervised learning analyzes unlabeled data to identify patterns, group samples by similarity (clustering), or project features into lower dimensions.
usage: Examples of unsupervised learning: K-Means clustering, PCA, DBSCAN, Gaussian Mixture Models.
spanish: Para descubrir estructuras ocultas, agrupaciones o reducir dimensiones sin utilizar etiquetas de referencia reales, aplicas aprendizaje no supervisado (unsupervised learning).

text: To train autonomous agents to make sequences of decisions by interacting with an environment, we use {{c1::reinforcement learning}} based on rewards.
explanation: In Reinforcement Learning (RL), an agent learns to maximize cumulative reward by executing actions in an environment and observing states and outcomes (Markov Decision Process).
usage: Key concepts in RL: Agent, Environment, State, Action, Reward, Q-Learning, Policy Gradient.
spanish: Para entrenar agentes autónomos para tomar secuencias de decisiones mediante la interacción con un entorno, utilizamos el aprendizaje por refuerzo (reinforcement learning) basado en recompensas.

text: When a model learns noise and specific details of the training data too well, it suffers from {{c1::overfitting}} (high variance).
explanation: Overfitting occurs when a model is overly complex relative to the simplicity of the data. It performs exceptionally well on training data but generalizes poorly to unseen test data.
usage: To prevent overfitting: use regularization (L1/L2), cross-validation, dropouts, or gather more training data.
spanish: Cuando un modelo aprende el ruido y los detalles específicos de los datos de entrenamiento demasiado bien, sufre de sobreajuste (overfitting - alta varianza).

text: If a model is too simple to capture the underlying pattern of the data, it yields high bias, a state called {{c1::underfitting}}.
explanation: Underfitting occurs when the model complexity is insufficient (e.g. fitting a straight line to quadratic data). The model performs poorly on both training and validation sets.
usage: To fix underfitting: increase model complexity, add feature interactions, or decrease regularization parameters.
spanish: Si un modelo es demasiado simple para capturar el patrón subyacente de los datos, produce un sesgo alto, un estado llamado subajuste (underfitting).

text: Finding the optimal model complexity that minimizes both generalization error sources is known as the {{c1::bias-variance tradeoff}}.
explanation: Total error can be decomposed into Bias error (underfitting), Variance error (overfitting), and Irreducible error (noise). As model complexity increases, bias decreases but variance increases.
usage: Optimal model complexity is located at the minimum of the U-shaped validation error curve.
spanish: Encontrar la complejidad óptima del modelo que minimiza ambas fuentes de error de generalización se conoce como el equilibrio entre sesgo y varianza (bias-variance tradeoff).

text: To evaluate a model's generalization capability robustly, you partition the dataset using {{c1::K-Fold cross-validation}}.
explanation: K-Fold cross-validation divides the dataset into K subsets (folds). The model is trained K times, each time using K-1 folds for training and the remaining fold for validation, averaging the metrics.
usage: In Scikit-Learn: from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score; scores = cross_val_score(model, X, y, cv=5).
spanish: Para evaluar la capacidad de generalización de un modelo de forma robusta, particionas el conjunto de datos mediante validación cruzada de K particiones (K-Fold cross-validation).

text: When information from outside the training dataset is accidentally used to train the model, it causes {{c1::data leakage}}, yielding overly optimistic validation metrics.
explanation: Data leakage occurs when target or future information is mixed into the training set (e.g. scaling features before splitting data, or including target-proxies). The model performs perfectly in testing but fails in production.
usage: To prevent leakage: build pipelines that split data before any preprocessing or feature scaling steps.
spanish: Cuando la información de fuera del conjunto de datos de entrenamiento se utiliza accidentalmente para entrenar el modelo, causa filtración de datos (data leakage), produciendo métricas de validación demasiado optimistas.
